question: A user task in a Camunda 8.8 BPMN diagram is linked to a Camunda Form in Web Modeler. The form designer updates the form's schema. What must the diagram author do for the updated form to be used the next time the diagram is deployed?

options.text: Manually re-link the form to the user task each time the form changes.
Edit a JSON configuration on the user task to point at the new form version.
Nothing — the diagram always resolves to the latest form and the form is auto-deployed with it.
Detach and then re-attach the form binding before clicking Deploy.

a.text: Manual re-linking on every form change is exactly what the 8.4 improvement removed from the workflow; it is no longer required.

b.text: Editing a JSON configuration is not required — the docs explicitly call out that no manual JSON configuration is needed for linked forms anymore.

c.text: Since the 8.4 improvements, diagrams always have the latest form updates, no manual re-link or JSON configuration is needed, and linked forms are automatically deployed with the diagram.

d.text: Detach/re-attach is not part of the documented workflow; the link automatically resolves to the latest form version.

explanation: The 8.4 improvements to linked-form handling in Web Modeler — still in effect in 8.8 — mean a diagram's linked form always reflects the latest saved form, no manual re-linking or JSON configuration is needed, and linked forms are automatically deployed together with the diagram.
